headline: Trump faces a painful political reality, from Iran stalemate to legal setbacks
description: Six months ahead of the US midterm elections, the political momentum has turned sharply against President Donald Trump.

headline: Laure Ferrari, the Frenchwoman whispering in Nigel Farage's ear
Russia stands by Mali's military junta at all costs
'The wars in Ukraine and Iran have made the revival of nuclear power an undeniable necessity'
description: The implementation of nuclear revival projects must not obscure the fact that nuclear energy is not just like any other energy source, especially at a time when ongoing conflicts reveal just how easily critical infrastructure can become military targets.

headline: Second unexploded shell found at illegal French rave, says interior minister
description: On Saturday evening, bomb disposal experts intervened after a first shell was discovered close to the party site near Bourges, where tens of thousands from France and neighboring countries gathered this weekend.

headline: Craving kouign-amann? Five standout bakeries in Brittany
description: From Douarnenez to Rennes, Le Monde toured Brittany's bakeries and pastry shops offering the most tempting takes on this beloved cake enjoyed around the globe.

headline: 'Taiwan has become essential for the world's future,' says François Wu, deputy foreign minister
description: Two weeks before US President Donald Trump is due to visit Beijing, on May 14 and 15, Taiwanese Deputy Foreign Minister François Wu Chih-chung emphasized Taiwan's increasingly strategic position and the 'useful' global role it can play.

headline: Working while going through IVF: 'I didn't want my employers to know about it because of my career'

In a small French village, a father is charged with sexual abuse of 34 victims aged 3 to 9
description: A tree, a rope, a letter. After surviving a suicide attempt, a man revealed he had committed numerous crimes against children. After a year-long and controversial investigation, he was charged, on April 10, with rape and sexual assault of minors and recording child pornography.
byline: Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Lorraine de Foucher

headline: 'He grabbed me by the neck': Women cyclists speak out about violence and sexual harassment
description: Amid tense encounters between drivers and cyclists in city centers and with accidents on the rise, women regularly report experiencing sexist assaults. This has even caused some to give up cycling altogether.

headline: French authors face a reckoning
description: Nearly 200 writers have expressed their desire to leave the Grasset publishing house since the dismissal of its chief, Olivier Nora. This mass departure has highlighted their dependence on the publisher, especially regarding rights.

headline: 'Céline Dion will generate the equivalent of one-fifth of the economic impact of the Paris Olympics'
